title: Spicy Polish Tofu Bowl
description: A quick, spicy tofu bowl inspired by Polish pierogi flavors, finished with a zesty sesame sauce for a vegan-friendly, lazy Sunday meal.
instructions: Rinse rice, cook in 2 cups water until fluffy, set aside.
Blanch broccoli: bring water to boil, add broccoli 2 min, then transfer to ice bath and drain.
Press tofu: wrap in paper towels and press for 15 minutes, then cut into 1 cm cubes.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium heat, fry tofu until golden on all sides, remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add a splash of oil and stir‑fry carrot, bell pepper, and green onions for 2–3 minutes.
Add blanched broccoli, toss, and remove from heat.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sriracha, sesame oil, sugar (or maple syrup), and 1 tbsp water in a small bowl; pour into skillet and stir to coat everything.
Return tofu to the skillet and combine with the sauce and vegetables.
Serve over cooked jasmine rice, sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds, sliced pickled daikon, and extra green onions. Enjoy!
